text: Welcome to This is my Architecture. Today my guest is Dennis Jost from Define Media. Welcome Dennis.
Hello. So Dennis, tell me a little bit about Define Media. What's your business? What are you doing?
Define Media is an online marketer for advertisements in the internet and what we do
is we play out the ads according to their context, optimal for our customers.
Okay, so is there anything special about the approach you have chosen?
We use machine learning in order to precise predictions about the click-through rate and use this knowledge in order to play out the best ad.
Okay, machine learning. That sounds interesting. So how does this work out? Show me a little bit through your architecture.
The main component is this Decision Lambda worker. It is doing about 100,000 predictions per minute.
Wow. Yes, and it is connected to the ad script, which is running on the browser of the user.
And from there on we can gather more data about interactions with the ad, which helps us in and which are stored into the S3 bucket and used for business analytics.
And of course to train our network at the end.
Okay, cool. So what kind of data does this machine learning part in the Lambda function need to make its decisions?
Okay. So mostly the measurements from here.
Okay. Was it a click or not?
This is the target size we predict.
But of course we need some more information about the pages, where the ad is played, about the placement, where on the page, where was it placed.
And of course we have some additional information about the location, where the user was when the ad was played.
And this dimension data is enriched with AKS and then put in the data lake as well.
Okay. So typically machine learning needs a little bit of time to compute and for advertisement business, you need to get a really low latency.
That's my understanding. So what's the secret sauce you are applying here to make it fast?
So actually we divided the inference of the neural network into two steps.
At first in the AKS, we just embed the data into embeddings that we can use in the Lambda worker to really
do fast decisions.
Okay.
We only have to execute then the head of the network and the rest is done in the AKS.
Okay. Awesome.
Okay, Dennis. So what's on your wish list for AWS? What are you planning to use in the future?
Of course, we are looking very forward to the H100 instances. These are the new GPU instances we can use to train our neural network even faster.
Dennis, thanks for sharing. That was very insightful.
I'm looking forward to what you are going to accomplish in the future. So thanks a lot.
Thank you very much.
And thanks for watching. This is my architecture.
